subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF Art IV 2026, OECD, EC, Eurostat, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem) + Czech official (CZSO, Czech National Bank) + Czech independent press (Radio Prague, plus Seznam Zpravy/Denik N read indirectly).
Politics: the Oct-2025 election returned the Babis/ANO party in a populist-leaning coalition - a democratic, orderly transfer that raises EU-friction and alliance-recalibration risk.

Standout features: a prudent, export-driven economy, strong institutions, and low crime, against a populist political turn, a near-term defence/alliance haircut, and demographic decline. Source-lean: MODERATE Anglophone.

economic.notes: A prudent fiscal position, a credible central bank, sound banks, and a strong export base.

fiscal_state.notes: Low debt ~44.6% GDP with a deficit ~2% - prudent, with the IMF urging modest consolidation to hold the line. (IMF Art IV 2026)

monetary_independence.notes: The CNB is statutorily independent and highly credible (repo ~3.5%). (CNB)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The koruna (an EU non-eurozone currency) is not a reserve asset. (definitional)

inflation.notes: At or near the 2% target band, with a brief late-2026 uptick then converging. (CNB)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised and profitable, with deposits exceeding loans and 2025 stress tests passed. (CNB FSR)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: An export/auto/manufacturing powerhouse deeply integrated into German supply chains and a nearshoring beneficiary, no resource curse; concentration the flip-side risk. (OECD 2025; EC)

institutional.notes: Independent EU-compliant courts, credible statistics, and a free press, with media-ownership concentration the watch-item.

rule_of_law.notes: Independent, EU-compliant courts (a WJP top-20 standing), with the incoming prime minister's unresolved business conflict-of-interest the watch-item. (WJP 2025; EC)

statistical_integrity.notes: The CZSO is credible and Eurostat-compliant. (CZSO; Eurostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A capable administration with strong EU-funds absorption. (OECD)

press_freedom.notes: Free and diverse (an RSF top-tier standing), with high ownership concentration and prime-minister-linked media a structural concern. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: Strong liberties and an integrated refugee population, against a populist political turn and demographic decline.

political_stability.notes: The Oct-2025 election returned the Babis/ANO party in a coalition with populist/right partners - a populist turn raising EU-friction risk, but the transfer was democratic and orderly. (OSW; Euronews)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A large, lawfully integrated Ukrainian-refugee population on durable work/residence routes. (UNHCR; OSW)

social_cohesion.notes: Cohesive, with comparatively low polarisation despite the populist current. (V-Dem; OSW)

demographics.notes: Sub-replacement fertility (~1.37) and ageing, with migration only partly offsetting a deepening natural decline. (CZSO)

geopolitical.notes: A NATO/EU member with an open economy, against a near-term alliance recalibration and war-adjacency.

alliance_reliability.notes: A NATO/EU member, though an observable post-2025 recalibration (defence below the NATO pledge, scaled-back ammunition backing) trims near-term reliability. (NATO; Euronews)

conflict_involvement.notes: No direct conflict, war-adjacent in Central Europe with no border/frontline exposure. (observed)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: An EU regime with an open capital account. (EU)

physical_practical.notes: Good modern infrastructure, universal healthcare, and low crime, against flood exposure.

climate.notes: Temperate, high readiness, but recurrent drought (Elbe basin) and 2024 floods. (ND-GAIN; EEA)

healthcare.notes: Universal with high satisfaction and low unmet need, with a workforce-ageing risk. (OECD Health at a Glance 2025)

crime_safety.notes: Low crime, with the capital among the safest cities - a clear strength. (CZSO)

disaster_insurance.notes: Flood exposure in a developed but unevenly penetrated insurance market. (2024 floods)

summary: A prudently managed, export-driven economy - low debt, a credible central bank, sound banks, deep German supply-chain integration - with strong, EU-compliant institutions, an integrated Ukrainian-refugee population, low crime, and good infrastructure. The 2025 election produced a democratic but populist-leaning turn that raises EU-friction risk and an observable near-term defence/alliance recalibration, and the long-run demographic decline is a real drag. Media-ownership concentration and the incoming prime minister's business conflict-of-interest are the institutional watch-items.
